studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Measurements are collected at pre-treatment (T0), 3 months (T1), 6 months (T2), 9 months (T3), and 12 months (T4). The web-based program can be accessed for 365 days after registration.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Optimune (Experimental) and Care-as-Usual (Active Comparator / Wait list)
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The Optimune arm receives immediate 12-month access to a web-based psychological intervention (including CBT techniques, sleep/stress management, and lifestyle optimization) alongside care as usual. The Care-as-Usual arm continues any required treatment and receives access to Optimune after a 6-month delay.
studies.collected_data: Plasma concentration of C-reactive protein (CRP), circulating IL-6, circulating TNF-α, stimulated IL-6, stimulated TNF-α; Plasma Concentrations of Cytokines (IL-1β, IFN-α, IFN-γ, MCP-1, IL-8, IL-10, IL-12p70, IL-17A, IL-18, IL-23, IL-33); Circulating numbers of Lymphocytes, Monocytes, Granulocytes; Phenotypic analysis of T and NK cell subsets; Cortisol awakening response (CAR); Brief Fatigue Inventory Questionnaire (BFI-9); Impact of Event Scale (IES-R); Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9); Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD-7); Fear of progression (PA-F12); Usefulness of the program questionnaire; Inventory for the Assessment of Negative Effects of Psychotherapy (INEP); Recurrence of breast cancer; Frequency of common cold or virus flu; Frequency of unscheduled medical encounters.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.follow_up_after_primary_end_point: yes, at 6 months (and up to 12 months for emotional stress)
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary outcomes are assessed at 3 months post-baseline. Follow-up measurements are collected at 6 months (with emotional stress also assessed at 9 and 12 months). The web-based program can be accessed for 365 days after registration.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention: Optimune (web-based psychological intervention); Control: Care-as-Usual (wait list control)
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Optimune arm: Immediate 12-month access to a web-based psychological intervention (CBT, sleep/stress management, lifestyle optimization) plus Care-as-Usual. Care-as-Usual arm: Continues with any required treatment and receives access to Optimune 6 months post-baseline (wait list).
studies.collected_data: WHOQOL-BREF (Quality of Life), IPAQ-short (Physical Activity), FQQ (Diet Quality), BFI-9 (Brief Fatigue Inventory), IES-R (Cancer-related emotional stress), PHQ-9 (Depression), GAD-7 (Anxiety), PA-F12 (Fear of progression), ISI (Insomnia Severity Index), Usefulness of the program questionnaire, INEP (Inventory for the Assessment of Negative Effects of Psychotherapy), recurrence of breast cancer, frequency of common cold or virus flu, frequency of unscheduled medical encounters, Body Mass Index (BMI), and initiation of psychotherapy.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The online program is used for 3 months. The intervention group receives access to the program for 365 days.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: access to the online program optimune for 365 days in addition to routine treatment (care as usual). Control group: routine treatment (care as usual), access to the online program optimune for 365 days after completion of the study.
studies.collected_data: Assessment of Quality of Life (AQoL-8D) total score, subscales, dimensions, and responder rate; Depression-Anxiety-Stress-Scales (DASS-21); Work and Social Adjustment Scale (WSAS); Brief Fatigue Inventory (BFI).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention group received 12-month access to Optimune; the control group received Optimune after a delay of 3 months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received care as usual (CAU) plus 12-month access to the Optimune Internet intervention immediately after randomization. Control group received CAU and access to Optimune after a delay of 3 months.
studies.collected_data: Quality of life (QoL), physical activity / exercise, and dietary habits.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Intention-to-treat (ITT) analyses revealed significant effects on QoL (d = 0.27, 95% CI: 0.07-0.48) and dietary habits (d = 0.36, 95% CI: 0.15-0.56), but the effect on physical exercise was not significant (d = 0.30; 95% CI: 0.10-0.51). The findings suggest the effectiveness of Optimune in facilitating improvements in quality of life and dietary habits.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Intervention group received 12-month access to Optimune immediately; control group received it after a 3-month delay.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received care as usual (CAU) plus 12-month access to Optimune immediately after randomization. Control group received CAU and Optimune after a delay of 3 months.
studies.collected_data: Quality of life (QoL), physical activity, and dietary habits. Instruments referenced in the text include WHOQOL-BREF, International Physical Activity Questionnaire Short Form (IPAQ-SF), Brief Fatigue Inventory, Impact of Event Scale-Revised, PHQ-9, Generalized Anxiety Disorder Screener (GAD-7), and Insomnia Severity Index.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Intention-to-treat (ITT) analyses revealed significant effects on QoL (d = 0.27, 95% CI: 0.07–0.48) and dietary habits (d = 0.36, 95% CI: 0.15–0.56), but the effect on physical exercise was not significant (d = 0.30; 95% CI: 0.10–0.51).
